question.english: For parents, what is important when bringing up their children?
question.chinese: 對父母來說，在養育孩子時，甚麼是重要的？

source_lines.text: Topic: For parents, what is important when bringing up their children?
對父母來說，在養育孩子時，甚麼是重要的？

components.english: I think one of the most important things is to give children both love and guidance, because they need warmth on the one hand and direction on the other.
components.chinese: 我認為，其中一個最重要的事情，就是同時給孩子愛和引導，因為他們一方面需要溫暖，另一方面也需要方向。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one of the most important things is to give children both love
and guidance, because they need warmth on the one hand and direction on the
other.
[我認為，其中一個最重要的事情，就是同時給孩子愛和引導，因為他們一方面需要溫暖，另
一方面也需要方向。]

components.english: The main reason is that children do not grow well on affection alone, and they do not grow well on rules alone either. If parents are loving but give no boundaries, a child may feel lost; if they are strict without warmth, the child may feel afraid rather than secure. In my view, good parenting is a bit like giving a child both roots and a compass: roots to feel safe, and a compass to know how to move through life.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，孩子單靠被愛其實不足以健康成長，而單靠規矩也一樣不夠。如果父母很有愛，卻完全沒有界線，孩子可能會感到迷失；但如果父母只有嚴格，沒有溫度，孩子又可能感到害怕，而不是踏實。在我看來，好的養育方式有點像同時給孩子根和羅盤：根讓他有安全感，羅盤則讓他知道怎樣走人生的路。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that children do not grow well on affection
alone, and they do not grow well on rules alone either. If parents are loving but
give no boundaries, a child may feel lost; if they are strict without warmth, the
child may feel afraid rather than secure. In my view, good parenting is a bit like
giving a child both roots and a compass: roots to feel safe, and a compass to
know how to move through life.
[主要原因是，孩子單靠被愛其實不足以健康成長，而單靠規矩也一樣不夠。如果父母很有
愛，卻完全沒有界線，孩子可能會感到迷失；但如果父母只有嚴格，沒有溫度，孩子又可能
感到害怕，而不是踏實。在我看來，好的養育方式有點像同時給孩子根和羅盤：根讓他有安
全感，羅盤則讓他知道怎樣走人生的路。]

components.english: For example, a good parent may listen patiently when a child is upset, but also teach them to be polite, keep promises, and take responsibility for their actions. In daily life, that might mean comforting them when they fail a test, while still reminding them that effort and discipline matter.
components.chinese: 例如，一位好的父母也許會在孩子難過時耐心聆聽，但同時也會教他有禮貌、守信用，以及為自己的行為負責。在日常生活中，這可能就是在孩子考試失手時先安慰他，但同時仍然提醒他，努力和自律同樣重要。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a good parent may listen patiently when a child is
upset, but also teach them to be polite, keep promises, and take responsibility
for their actions. In daily life, that might mean comforting them when they fail
a test, while still reminding them that effort and discipline matter.
[例如，一位好的父母也許會在孩子難過時耐心聆聽，但同時也會教他有禮貌、守信用，以及
為自己的行為負責。在日常生活中，這可能就是在孩子考試失手時先安慰他，但同時仍然提
醒他，努力和自律同樣重要。]

components.english: So, if I had to sum it up, I would say what matters most is raising children in a way that is both caring and consistent.
components.chinese: 所以，如果要我總結，我會說最重要的是用一種既關愛、又有一致性的方式去養育孩子。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to sum it up, I would say what matters most is raising
children in a way that is both caring and consistent.
[所以，如果要我總結，我會說最重要的是用一種既關愛、又有一致性的方式去養育孩子。]

components.english: On top of that, I think parents need to lead by example and gradually teach children independence, because children learn as much from what they see as from what they are told.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為父母需要以身作則，並且逐步培養孩子的獨立性，因為孩子從他們看見的東西學到的，很多時並不比從被告知的東西少。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think parents need to lead by example and gradually
teach children independence, because children learn as much from what they see
as from what they are told.
[除此之外，我也認為父母需要以身作則，並且逐步培養孩子的獨立性，因為孩子從他們看
見的東西學到的，很多時並不比從被告知的東西少。]

components.english: To be honest, children are like little mirrors: they pick up habits, attitudes, and ways of speaking from the adults around them. If parents talk about honesty, patience, or respect but do not practise those things themselves, the message can fall flat. At the same time, children should not be wrapped in cotton wool. They need chances to make small decisions, solve simple problems, and learn from mistakes, otherwise they may grow up dependent and unsure of themselves.
components.chinese: 老實說，孩子有點像一面小鏡子：他們會吸收身邊大人的習慣、態度，以及說話方式。如果父母口裡一直說誠實、耐性，或者尊重很重要，但自己卻做不到，那些道理很多時就會失去力量。同時，孩子也不應該被保護得太過頭。他們需要有機會作出一些小決定、處理一些簡單問題，以及從錯誤中學習，否則長大後可能會變得依賴，也對自己缺乏信心。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, children are like little mirrors: they pick up
habits, attitudes, and ways of speaking from the adults around them. If parents
talk about honesty, patience, or respect but do not practise those things
themselves, the message can fall flat. At the same time, children should not be
wrapped in cotton wool. They need chances to make small decisions, solve
simple problems, and learn from mistakes, otherwise they may grow up
dependent and unsure of themselves.
[老實說，孩子有點像一面小鏡子：他們會吸收身邊大人的習慣、態度，以及說話方式。如果
父母口裡一直說誠實、耐性，或者尊重很重要，但自己卻做不到，那些道理很多時就會失去
力量。同時，孩子也不應該被保護得太過頭。他們需要有機會作出一些小決定、處理一些簡
單問題，以及從錯誤中學習，否則長大後可能會變得依賴，也對自己缺乏信心。]

components.english: For instance, if parents want a child to be responsible with money, they should not just lecture them about spending wisely; they can show it through everyday behaviour and perhaps let the child manage a small allowance. In the same way, if they want the child to become confident, they can encourage them to speak up, make choices, and deal with minor setbacks instead of stepping in every single time.
components.chinese: 例如，如果父母希望孩子對金錢有責任感，他們不應該只是一味說教要怎樣明智消費；他們可以透過日常行為示範，甚至讓孩子自己管理一點零用錢。同樣地，如果他們希望孩子變得有自信，也可以鼓勵孩子表達自己、作出選擇，並處理一些小挫折，而不是每一次都立刻替他出手解決。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if parents want a child to be responsible with money,
they should not just lecture them about spending wisely; they can show it
through everyday behaviour and perhaps let the child manage a small allowance.
In the same way, if they want the child to become confident, they can encourage
them to speak up, make choices, and deal with minor setbacks instead of
stepping in every single time.
[例如，如果父母希望孩子對金錢有責任感，他們不應該只是一味說教要怎樣明智消費；他
們可以透過日常行為示範，甚至讓孩子自己管理一點零用錢。同樣地，如果他們希望孩子變
得有自信，也可以鼓勵孩子表達自己、作出選擇，並處理一些小挫折，而不是每一次都立刻
替他出手解決。]

components.english: Overall, I think what is most important for parents is to provide love, clear values, good example, and the space for a child to grow into their own person. In the end, bringing up a child is not about controlling every step they take, but about helping them stand steadily on their own two feet.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為對父母來說，最重要的是提供愛、清晰的價值觀、良好的榜樣，以及讓孩子慢慢長成自己的空間。說到底，養育孩子並不是要控制他走的每一步，而是要幫助他最終能夠穩穩地站在自己的雙腳上。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I think what is most important for parents is to
provide love, clear values, good example, and the space for a child to grow into
their own person. In the end, bringing up a child is not about controlling every
step they take, but about helping them stand steadily on their own two feet.
[總結來說，我認為對父母來說，最重要的是提供愛、清晰的價值觀、良好的榜樣，以及讓孩
子慢慢長成自己的空間。說到底，養育孩子並不是要控制他走的每一步，而是要幫助他最終
能夠穩穩地站在自己的雙腳上。]

question.english: Do you think mothers and fathers have different roles to play in bringing up a child?
question.chinese: 你認為母親和父親在養育孩子方面扮演的角色是否不同？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think mothers and fathers have different roles to play in bringing
up a child?
你認為母親和父親在養育孩子方面扮演的角色是否不同？

components.english: Yes, I do think mothers and fathers often play somewhat different roles in bringing up a child, but I would not say those roles have to be fixed or old-fashioned.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為母親和父親在養育孩子時，很多時會扮演略有不同的角色；不過，我並不認為那些角色一定要是固定的，或者停留在很傳統的框架裡。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think mothers and fathers often play somewhat different roles
in bringing up a child, but I would not say those roles have to be fixed or
old-fashioned.
[是的，我的確認為母親和父親在養育孩子時，很多時會扮演略有不同的角色；不過，我並
不認為那些角色一定要是固定的，或者停留在很傳統的框架裡。]

components.english: The main reason is that different parents often bring different strengths, personalities, and ways of showing care. One parent may be more gentle and emotionally expressive, while the other may be more playful, practical, or firm. In that sense, raising a child is a bit like building a house: love is the foundation, but children also need guidance, boundaries, reassurance, and stability. Very often, the two parents contribute to that in slightly different ways.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，不同的家長往往會帶來不同的長處、性格，以及表達關愛的方式。某一位家長可能比較溫柔，也較擅長表達情感；另一位則可能更有玩心、更實際，或者在原則上更堅定。從這個角度來說，養育孩子有點像蓋一間房子：愛是地基，但孩子同時也需要引導、界線、安全感，以及穩定感。而很多時候，兩位家長正正就是用略有不同的方式去提供這些東西。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that different parents often bring different
strengths, personalities, and ways of showing care. One parent may be more
gentle and emotionally expressive, while the other may be more playful,
practical, or firm. In that sense, raising a child is a bit like building a house: love
is the foundation, but children also need guidance, boundaries, reassurance, and
stability. Very often, the two parents contribute to that in slightly different
ways.
[主要原因是，不同的家長往往會帶來不同的長處、性格，以及表達關愛的方式。某一位家
長可能比較溫柔，也較擅長表達情感；另一位則可能更有玩心、更實際，或者在原則上更堅
定。從這個角度來說，養育孩子有點像蓋一間房子：愛是地基，但孩子同時也需要引導、界
線、安全感，以及穩定感。而很多時候，兩位家長正正就是用略有不同的方式去提供這些東

components.english: For example, if a child has had a bad day at school, one parent might naturally focus on comforting them and helping them talk through their feelings, while the other might encourage them to face the problem and figure out what to do next time. Both responses are valuable, because one soothes the heart and the other strengthens the spine.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子在學校過得不順利，其中一位家長可能會很自然地先安慰他，並幫他把情緒說出來；而另一位則可能會鼓勵他正面面對問題，想想下次可以怎樣做。這兩種回應其實都很有價值，因為前者安撫的是情緒，後者培養的則是韌性。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child has had a bad day at school, one parent might
naturally focus on comforting them and helping them talk through their
feelings, while the other might encourage them to face the problem and figure
out what to do next time. Both responses are valuable, because one soothes the
heart and the other strengthens the spine.
[例如，如果一個孩子在學校過得不順利，其中一位家長可能會很自然地先安慰他，並幫他
把情緒說出來；而另一位則可能會鼓勵他正面面對問題，想想下次可以怎樣做。這兩種回應
其實都很有價值，因為前者安撫的是情緒，後者培養的則是韌性。]

components.english: So yes, I would say mothers and fathers often do play different roles, but ideally those roles should complement each other rather than follow a rigid script.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說母親和父親很多時的確會扮演不同角色；但理想的情況是，這些角色應該彼此互補，而不是死板地照著某一套劇本去演。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say mothers and fathers often do play different
roles, but ideally those roles should complement each other rather than follow a
rigid script.
[所以，是的，我會說母親和父親很多時的確會扮演不同角色；但理想的情況是，這些角色
應該彼此互補，而不是死板地照著某一套劇本去演。]

components.english: That said, I do not believe good parenting depends strictly on gender, because what matters most is whether the adults are loving, responsible, and emotionally present.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為好的育兒一定嚴格取決於性別，因為真正重要的，是那些大人是否有愛心、負責任，以及在情感上真正陪伴著孩子。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not believe good parenting depends strictly on gender,
because what matters most is whether the adults are loving, responsible, and
emotionally present.
[不過，我並不認為好的育兒一定嚴格取決於性別，因為真正重要的，是那些大人是否有愛
心、負責任，以及在情感上真正陪伴著孩子。]

components.english: In modern families, mothers can be firm and career-driven, and fathers can be deeply nurturing and hands-on. To be honest, children do not need a mother to fit one stereotype and a father to fit another. What they really need is a steady adult presence. If both parents can listen, guide, and set boundaries, then the child is far more likely to grow up feeling secure. In other words, it is less about who plays which role on paper, and more about whether the child is surrounded by care and consistency.
components.chinese: 在現代家庭裡，母親可以很有原則，也可以很重視事業；父親同樣可以非常細心、充滿照顧性，而且真正參與育兒。老實說，孩子並不需要一位母親去符合某一種刻板印象，也不需要一位父親去符合另一種。他們真正需要的，是穩定而可靠的大人陪伴。如果父母雙方都能夠聆聽、引導，並設立界線，那孩子就更有可能在安全感之中成長。換句話說，重點不在於表面上誰扮演哪個角色，而在於孩子是否被關愛和一致性包圍著。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: In modern families, mothers can be firm and career-driven,
and fathers can be deeply nurturing and hands-on. To be honest, children do
not need a mother to fit one stereotype and a father to fit another. What they
really need is a steady adult presence. If both parents can listen, guide, and set
boundaries, then the child is far more likely to grow up feeling secure. In other
words, it is less about who plays which role on paper, and more about whether
the child is surrounded by care and consistency.
[在現代家庭裡，母親可以很有原則，也可以很重視事業；父親同樣可以非常細心、充滿照
顧性，而且真正參與育兒。老實說，孩子並不需要一位母親去符合某一種刻板印象，也不需
要一位父親去符合另一種。他們真正需要的，是穩定而可靠的大人陪伴。如果父母雙方都能
夠聆聽、引導，並設立界線，那孩子就更有可能在安全感之中成長。換句話說，重點不在於
表面上誰扮演哪個角色，而在於孩子是否被關愛和一致性包圍著。]

components.english: For instance, in some families, the father may be the one who cooks, helps with homework, and talks about feelings, while the mother may be the one who handles discipline, planning, and decision-making. If the child is thriving, then clearly the old labels do not matter very much.
components.chinese: 例如，在某些家庭裡，父親可能是那位負責煮飯、陪做功課，以及和孩子談感受的人；而母親則可能是那位負責管教、安排事情，以及作決定的人。如果那個孩子成長得很好，那就很清楚地說明：那些舊有標籤其實並沒有那麼重要。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, in some families, the father may be the one who
cooks, helps with homework, and talks about feelings, while the mother may be
the one who handles discipline, planning, and decision-making. If the child is
thriving, then clearly the old labels do not matter very much.
[例如，在某些家庭裡，父親可能是那位負責煮飯、陪做功課，以及和孩子談感受的人；而母
親則可能是那位負責管教、安排事情，以及作決定的人。如果那個孩子成長得很好，那就很
清楚地說明：那些舊有標籤其實並沒有那麼重要。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say mothers and fathers often bring different qualities to raising a child, and that can be a real strength. In the end, what matters most is not whether they divide their roles in a traditional way, but whether they can work together to give a child love, guidance, and a sense of security.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說母親和父親在養育孩子時，往往會帶來不同的特質，而這其實可以成為一種真正的優勢。說到底，最重要的並不是他們是否按照傳統方式去分工，而是他們能否一起給孩子愛、引導和安全感。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say mothers and fathers often bring different
qualities to raising a child, and that can be a real strength. In the end, what
matters most is not whether they divide their roles in a traditional way, but
whether they can work together to give a child love, guidance, and a sense of
security.
[總結來說，我會說母親和父親在養育孩子時，往往會帶來不同的特質，而這其實可以成為
一種真正的優勢。說到底，最重要的並不是他們是否按照傳統方式去分工，而是他們能否一
起給孩子愛、引導和安全感。]

question.english: Do you think hitting children is sometimes necessary for discipline?
question.chinese: 你認為為了管教孩子，有時候打他們是有必要的嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think hitting children is sometimes necessary for discipline?
你認為為了管教孩子，有時候打他們是有必要的嗎？

components.english: No, I do not think hitting children is necessary for discipline.
components.chinese: 不，我並不認為為了管教孩子，打他們是有必要的。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: No, I do not think hitting children is necessary for discipline.
[不，我並不認為為了管教孩子，打他們是有必要的。]

components.english: It may stop a child in the moment because of shock or fear, but that is not the same as real discipline. Real discipline is supposed to teach self-control, understanding, and better behaviour over time. Major child-health bodies say physical punishment does not improve behaviour in the long run and is linked to harms such as increased aggression, anxiety, and other negative outcomes.
components.chinese: 它也許會因為震懾或恐懼而令孩子當下停下來，但那並不等於真正的管教。真正的管教，應該是教會孩子自制、理解，以及長遠更好的行為。主要兒童健康機構都指出，體罰並不能在長遠上改善行為，反而和更高的攻擊性、焦慮，以及其他負面結果有關。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: It may stop a child in the moment because of shock or fear, but
that is not the same as real discipline. Real discipline is supposed to teach
self-control, understanding, and better behaviour over time. Major child-health
bodies say physical punishment does not improve behaviour in the long run and
is linked to harms such as increased aggression, anxiety, and other negative
outcomes.
[它也許會因為震懾或恐懼而令孩子當下停下來，但那並不等於真正的管教。真正的管教，
應該是教會孩子自制、理解，以及長遠更好的行為。主要兒童健康機構都指出，體罰並不能
在長遠上改善行為，反而和更高的攻擊性、焦慮，以及其他負面結果有關。]

components.english: For example, if a child hits their sibling and an adult responds by slapping the child, the child may go quiet, but the lesson they may absorb is that the stronger person gets to use force. A calmer response, such as stopping the behaviour, explaining the rule, and giving a clear consequence, teaches far more.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子打了自己的兄弟姊妹，而大人回應的方法是打回那個孩子，那麼孩子也許會安靜下來，但他真正吸收到的訊息，很可能只是「比較強的人可以用暴力」。相反，如果大人冷靜地先制止行為，再清楚解釋規則，並給出明確後果，這樣其實能教得更多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child hits their sibling and an adult responds by
slapping the child, the child may go quiet, but the lesson they may absorb is that
the stronger person gets to use force. A calmer response, such as stopping the
behaviour, explaining the rule, and giving a clear consequence, teaches far more.
[例如，如果一個孩子打了自己的兄弟姊妹，而大人回應的方法是打回那個孩子，那麼孩子
也許會安靜下來，但他真正吸收到的訊息，很可能只是「比較強的人可以用暴力」。相反，如
果大人冷靜地先制止行為，再清楚解釋規則，並給出明確後果，這樣其實能教得更多。]

components.english: So, to me, hitting may produce obedience on the surface, but it is a poor teacher underneath.
components.chinese: 所以，在我看來，打孩子也許表面上能換來服從，但它骨子裡其實是一個很差的老師。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, hitting may produce obedience on the surface, but it is
a poor teacher underneath.
[所以，在我看來，打孩子也許表面上能換來服從，但它骨子裡其實是一個很差的老師。]

components.english: That said, I do think children need firm boundaries and real consequences; I just do not think violence should be one of them.
components.chinese: 不過，我的確認為孩子需要清楚的界線和真正的後果；我只是不認為暴力應該成為其中一種方法。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think children need firm boundaries and real
consequences; I just do not think violence should be one of them.
[不過，我的確認為孩子需要清楚的界線和真正的後果；我只是不認為暴力應該成為其中一
種方法。]

components.english: Good discipline is not the same as being soft. In fact, positive discipline can be very firm. It means setting expectations, staying consistent, and responding in a way that protects the child’s dignity while still making the rule clear. It is highly recommended that approaches like calm correction, limit-setting, redirection, and positive reinforcement be adopted rather than hitting or shaming.
components.chinese: 好的管教並不等於心軟。事實上，正向管教也可以很有原則。它的意思是，要訂立清楚期望、保持一致性，並用一種既能維護孩子尊嚴、又能把規則說清楚的方式去回應。建議使用冷靜糾正、設限、轉移注意力，以及正向強化這類方法，而不是打罵或羞辱。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Good discipline is not the same as being soft. In fact, positive
discipline can be very firm. It means setting expectations, staying consistent,
and responding in a way that protects the child’s dignity while still making the
rule clear. It is highly recommended that approaches like calm correction,
limit-setting, redirection, and positive reinforcement be adopted rather than
hitting or shaming.
[好的管教並不等於心軟。事實上，正向管教也可以很有原則。它的意思是，要訂立清楚期
望、保持一致性，並用一種既能維護孩子尊嚴、又能把規則說清楚的方式去回應。建議使用
冷靜糾正、設限、轉移注意力，以及正向強化這類方法，而不是打罵或羞辱。]

components.english: For instance, if a child is about to run into the road, of course an adult should grab them immediately. But that is protection, not punishment. After the danger has passed, the adult can explain the rule firmly and make the consequence clear. That way, the child learns safety without being taught fear as a language of love.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子正要衝出馬路，成年人當然應該立刻把他拉住。但那是保護，不是懲罰。等危險過去之後，大人就可以堅定地解釋規則，並把後果說清楚。這樣一來，孩子學到的是安全，而不是把恐懼誤認成關愛的語言。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a child is about to run into the road, of course an
adult should grab them immediately. But that is protection, not punishment.
After the danger has passed, the adult can explain the rule firmly and make the
consequence clear. That way, the child learns safety without being taught fear as
a language of love.
[例如，如果一個孩子正要衝出馬路，成年人當然應該立刻把他拉住。但那是保護，不是懲
罰。等危險過去之後，大人就可以堅定地解釋規則，並把後果說清楚。這樣一來，孩子學到
的是安全，而不是把恐懼誤認成關愛的語言。]

components.english: Overall, I would say hitting children is not necessary for discipline, and in most cases it is more harmful than helpful. Firm, calm, respectful discipline may take more patience, but it teaches the right lesson and leaves fewer scars, both seen and unseen.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說打孩子並不是管教所必需的，而且在大多數情況下，它帶來的傷害往往比幫助更多。堅定、冷靜而有尊重的管教，也許需要更多耐性，但它教的是正確的功課，而且留下的傷痕會少得多，無論是看得見的，還是看不見的。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say hitting children is not necessary for
discipline, and in most cases it is more harmful than helpful. Firm, calm,
respectful discipline may take more patience, but it teaches the right lesson and
leaves fewer scars, both seen and unseen.
[總結來說，我會說打孩子並不是管教所必需的，而且在大多數情況下，它帶來的傷害往往
比幫助更多。堅定、冷靜而有尊重的管教，也許需要更多耐性，但它教的是正確的功課，而
且留下的傷痕會少得多，無論是看得見的，還是看不見的。]

question.english: Do you think sweets are a good thing to reward children with?
question.chinese: 你認為用糖果或甜食來獎勵小朋友是好事嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think sweets are a good thing to reward children with?
你認為用糖果或甜食來獎勵小朋友是好事嗎？

components.english: Personally, I would not say sweets are the best reward overall, although a small treat once in a while is probably fine.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我不會說甜食是最好的獎勵方式，不過偶爾給一點小小的甜點，我覺得問題不大。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I would not say sweets are the best reward overall, although a
small treat once in a while is probably fine.
[就我個人而言，我不會說甜食是最好的獎勵方式，不過偶爾給一點小小的甜點，我覺得問
題不大。]

components.english: The main reason is that if children are rewarded with sweets too often, they may start to see sugary food as something special, emotional, or even deserved. That can be a bit of a slippery slope. Instead of learning that sweets are just occasional treats, they may begin to connect them with praise, comfort, or success. In other words, the sweet stops being just food and starts becoming a kind of trophy.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，如果小朋友太常用甜食作為獎勵，他們可能會開始把高糖食物看成某種特別的、帶情緒價值的，甚至是「自己應得」的東西。這其實有點容易一路滑下去。孩子本來應該學會的是：甜食只是偶爾吃的零食；但慢慢地，他們卻可能把它和被稱讚、被安慰，或者成功連在一起。換句話說，那份甜食就不再只是食物，而開始變成一種獎盃。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that if children are rewarded with sweets too
often, they may start to see sugary food as something special, emotional, or even
deserved. That can be a bit of a slippery slope. Instead of learning that sweets
are just occasional treats, they may begin to connect them with praise, comfort,
or success. In other words, the sweet stops being just food and starts becoming a
kind of trophy.
[主要原因是，如果小朋友太常用甜食作為獎勵，他們可能會開始把高糖食物看成某種特別
的、帶情緒價值的，甚至是「自己應得」的東西。這其實有點容易一路滑下去。孩子本來應該
學會的是：甜食只是偶爾吃的零食；但慢慢地，他們卻可能把它和被稱讚、被安慰，或者成功
連在一起。換句話說，那份甜食就不再只是食物，而開始變成一種獎盃。]

components.english: For example, if a child is told, “Finish your homework and I’ll give you chocolate,” they may do the task, of course, but they may also start expecting sugar every time they behave well. After a while, good behaviour is no longer its own reward; it comes with a price tag.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子被告訴：「做完功課就給你朱古力」，他當然可能會把事情做好；但同時，他也可能開始期待自己每次表現好時，都應該有甜食。久而久之，良好行為本身就不再是值得做的事，而像是附帶了一個標價。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a child is told, “Finish your homework and I’ll give
you chocolate,” they may do the task, of course, but they may also start
expecting sugar every time they behave well. After a while, good behaviour is no
longer its own reward; it comes with a price tag.
[例如，如果一個孩子被告訴：「做完功課就給你朱古力」，他當然可能會把事情做好；但同時
，他也可能開始期待自己每次表現好時，都應該有甜食。久而久之，良好行為本身就不再是
值得做的事，而像是附帶了一個標價。]

components.english: So, in general, I would say sweets are not the most sensible reward, because they can create unhealthy habits and send the wrong message about food.
components.chinese: 所以，一般來說，我會說甜食並不是最理想的獎勵，因為它可能會養成不太健康的習慣，也會對食物傳遞出不太理想的訊息。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, in general, I would say sweets are not the most sensible
reward, because they can create unhealthy habits and send the wrong message
about food.
[所以，一般來說，我會說甜食並不是最理想的獎勵，因為它可能會養成不太健康的習慣，
也會對食物傳遞出不太理想的訊息。]

components.english: That said, I do not think sweets are a terrible thing in every single case, because moderation and context matter.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為甜食在任何情況下都一定很糟，因為分寸和情境其實很重要。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think sweets are a terrible thing in every single
case, because moderation and context matter.
[不過，我也不認為甜食在任何情況下都一定很糟，因為分寸和情境其實很重要。]

components.english: If it is only an occasional reward, and if the child already has a fairly balanced diet, then I do not think it is the end of the world. Childhood should not be so strict that every biscuit feels like a moral crisis. Sometimes a sweet can simply be a cheerful little gesture. The key, though, is that it should stay small and occasional, not become the go-to reward for every achievement, every good mood, and every quiet moment.
components.chinese: 如果那只是一種偶爾出現的獎勵，而且孩子本身的飲食已經算相當均衡，那我不覺得這就真的有那麼嚴重。童年也不應該嚴格到連一塊餅乾都像道德危機一樣。有時候，一點甜食真的就只是個讓人開心的小表示。不過，關鍵在於它必須維持在「小」和「偶爾」的範圍內，而不是變成每一次成就、每一次乖巧，甚至每一次安靜下來時的指定獎勵。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: If it is only an occasional reward, and if the child already has a
fairly balanced diet, then I do not think it is the end of the world. Childhood
should not be so strict that every biscuit feels like a moral crisis. Sometimes a
sweet can simply be a cheerful little gesture. The key, though, is that it should
stay small and occasional, not become the go-to reward for every achievement,
every good mood, and every quiet moment.
[如果那只是一種偶爾出現的獎勵，而且孩子本身的飲食已經算相當均衡，那我不覺得這就
真的有那麼嚴重。童年也不應該嚴格到連一塊餅乾都像道德危機一樣。有時候，一點甜食真
的就只是個讓人開心的小表示。不過，關鍵在於它必須維持在「小」和「偶爾」的範圍內，而不
是變成每一次成就、每一次乖巧，甚至每一次安靜下來時的指定獎勵。]

components.english: For instance, if a child has worked very hard for an exam or has shown real improvement in something difficult, giving them an ice cream once in a while is probably harmless. But I still think rewards like praise, extra playtime, a day out, or doing something fun together are healthier and often more meaningful in the long run.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個孩子為考試真的很努力，或者在某件困難的事上有明顯進步，那麼偶爾獎勵他一杯雪糕，大概也沒甚麼大問題。不過，我仍然覺得，像稱讚、多一點玩樂時間、一次外出活動，或者一家人一起做些有趣的事，這些獎勵方式會更健康，而且從長遠來看，往往也更有意義。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if a child has worked very hard for an exam or has
shown real improvement in something difficult, giving them an ice cream once
in a while is probably harmless. But I still think rewards like praise, extra
playtime, a day out, or doing something fun together are healthier and often
more meaningful in the long run.
[例如，如果一個孩子為考試真的很努力，或者在某件困難的事上有明顯進步，那麼偶爾獎
勵他一杯雪糕，大概也沒甚麼大問題。不過，我仍然覺得，像稱讚、多一點玩樂時間、一次外
出活動，或者一家人一起做些有趣的事，這些獎勵方式會更健康，而且從長遠來看，往往也
更有意義。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say sweets can be acceptable as an occasional treat, but they should not be the main way to reward children. The best reward is not turning sugar into a symbol of success, but helping children feel appreciated, encouraged, and genuinely proud of their own effort.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，甜食作為偶爾的小獎勵是可以接受的，但它不應該成為獎勵孩子的主要方式。最好的獎勵不是把糖分包裝成成功，而是讓孩子感受到被欣賞、被鼓勵，以及真正為自己的努力感到驕傲。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say sweets can be acceptable as an occasional treat,
but they should not be the main way to reward children. The best reward is not
turning sugar into a symbol of success, but helping children feel appreciated,
encouraged, and genuinely proud of their own effort.
[總結來說，我會說，甜食作為偶爾的小獎勵是可以接受的，但它不應該成為獎勵孩子的主
要方式。最好的獎勵不是把糖分包裝成成功，而是讓孩子感受到被欣賞、被鼓勵，以及真正
為自己的努力感到驕傲。]

question.english: Do you think parents spend too much on buying toys for their children?
question.chinese: 你認為父母在為孩子買玩具這方面花得太多嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think parents spend too much on buying toys for their children?
[你認為父母在為孩子買玩具這方面花得太多嗎？]

components.english: Yes, in many cases, I do think some parents spend more than they really need to on toys for their children.
components.chinese: 是的，在很多情況下，我的確認為有些父母在替孩子買玩具時，花的錢比真正需要的還要多。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, in many cases, I do think some parents spend more than they really
need to on toys for their children.
[是的，在很多情況下，我的確認為有些父母在替孩子買玩具時，花的錢比真正需要的還要

components.english: The main reason is that toys are often bought not only out of practicality, but also out of love, guilt, pressure, or the desire to keep children happy. Parents naturally want to give their children the best, but sometimes that can slide into overbuying. A child may be drawn to the newest shiny thing, yet lose interest almost as quickly as they got excited about it. In that sense, some parents are not really buying lasting value; they are buying a burst of excitement that fades very fast.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，玩具很多時候不只是基於實際需要而購買，也常常是出於愛、內疚、壓力，或者想讓孩子開心的心情。父母自然都想把最好的給孩子，但有時這種心態就會慢慢滑向買得太多。孩子也許會被最新、最吸引眼球的東西迷住，但那份興奮往往來得快，去得也快。從這個角度來說，有些父母買下的其實不是真正持久的價值，而是一陣很快就消散的興奮感。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that toys are often bought not only out of
practicality, but also out of love, guilt, pressure, or the desire to keep children
happy. Parents naturally want to give their children the best, but sometimes
that can slide into overbuying. A child may be drawn to the newest shiny thing,
yet lose interest almost as quickly as they got excited about it. In that sense,
some parents are not really buying lasting value; they are buying a burst of
excitement that fades very fast.
[主要原因是，玩具很多時候不只是基於實際需要而購買，也常常是出於愛、內疚、壓力，或
者想讓孩子開心的心情。父母自然都想把最好的給孩子，但有時這種心態就會慢慢滑向買
得太多。孩子也許會被最新、最吸引眼球的東西迷住，但那份興奮往往來得快，去得也快。
從這個角度來說，有些父母買下的其實不是真正持久的價值，而是一陣很快就消散的興奮

components.english: For example, some parents may buy a new toy every time they go shopping, or keep adding more toys at birthdays and festivals, even when the child already has plenty at home. Very often, the toy is played with for a few days and then quietly ends up in a corner, gathering dust.
components.chinese: 例如，有些父母每次去逛街都會替孩子買一件新玩具，或者到了生日和節日時又不斷加添新玩具，即使家裡其實已經有很多了。很多時候，那件玩具只會被玩幾天，然後便安安靜靜地被放到角落裡積塵。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, some parents may buy a new toy every time they go
shopping, or keep adding more toys at birthdays and festivals, even when the
child already has plenty at home. Very often, the toy is played with for a few
days and then quietly ends up in a corner, gathering dust.
[例如，有些父母每次去逛街都會替孩子買一件新玩具，或者到了生日和節日時又不斷加添
新玩具，即使家裡其實已經有很多了。很多時候，那件玩具只會被玩幾天，然後便安安靜靜
地被放到角落裡積塵。]

components.english: So yes, I would say some parents do spend too much on toys, especially when the buying becomes more habitual than meaningful.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說有些父母在玩具上確實花得太多，尤其是當購買已經變成一種習慣，而不再是一種真正有意義的選擇時。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say some parents do spend too much on toys,
especially when the buying becomes more habitual than meaningful.
[所以，是的，我會說有些父母在玩具上確實花得太多，尤其是當購買已經變成一種習慣，
而不再是一種真正有意義的選擇時。]

components.english: That said, I do not think spending money on toys is always a bad thing, because the right toys can genuinely help children learn and grow.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為在玩具上花錢就一定是壞事，因為合適的玩具的確可以真正幫助孩子學習和成長。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think spending money on toys is always a bad
thing, because the right toys can genuinely help children learn and grow.
[不過，我並不認為在玩具上花錢就一定是壞事，因為合適的玩具的確可以真正幫助孩子學
習和成長。]

components.english: The issue, to me, is not simply how much parents spend, but what they are spending on and why. Some toys encourage imagination, problem-solving, creativity, and social interaction, so they are much more than just entertainment. A well-chosen toy can open a door to learning, whereas a pile of random toys can just create clutter. In other words, it is a case of quality over quantity. Sometimes less really is more.
components.chinese: 對我來說，問題不只是父母花了多少錢，而是他們把錢花在甚麼東西上，以及背後原因是甚麼。有些玩具能培養想像力、解難能力、創造力，以及與人互動的能力，所以它們遠遠不只是娛樂而已。一件挑選得好的玩具可以為學習打開一扇門；但一大堆隨便買回來的玩具，卻可能只會製造雜亂。換句話說，這其實是重質不重量的問題。有時候，少一點反而更好。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The issue, to me, is not simply how much parents spend, but
what they are spending on and why. Some toys encourage imagination,
problem-solving, creativity, and social interaction, so they are much more than
just entertainment. A well-chosen toy can open a door to learning, whereas a
pile of random toys can just create clutter. In other words, it is a case of quality
over quantity. Sometimes less really is more.
[對我來說，問題不只是父母花了多少錢，而是他們把錢花在甚麼東西上，以及背後原因是
甚麼。有些玩具能培養想像力、解難能力、創造力，以及與人互動的能力，所以它們遠遠不
只是娛樂而已。一件挑選得好的玩具可以為學習打開一扇門；但一大堆隨便買回來的玩具，
卻可能只會製造雜亂。換句話說，這其實是重質不重量的問題。有時候，少一點反而更好。]

components.english: For instance, a puzzle, a set of building blocks, or an art kit may keep a child engaged for a long time and help develop useful skills at the same time. By contrast, ten flashy toys that break easily or lose their charm within a week may cost more but offer much less in the long run.
components.chinese: 例如，一盒拼圖、一套積木，或者一組美術材料，可能會讓孩子投入很長時間，同時也培養有用的能力。相比之下，十件外表很搶眼、卻很容易壞，或者一星期內就失去吸引力的玩具，雖然花的錢更多，但長遠來看帶來的價值反而少得多。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a puzzle, a set of building blocks, or an art kit may
keep a child engaged for a long time and help develop useful skills at the same
time. By contrast, ten flashy toys that break easily or lose their charm within a
week may cost more but offer much less in the long run.
[例如，一盒拼圖、一套積木，或者一組美術材料，可能會讓孩子投入很長時間，同時也培養
有用的能力。相比之下，十件外表很搶眼、卻很容易壞，或者一星期內就失去吸引力的玩具
，雖然花的錢更多，但長遠來看帶來的價值反而少得多。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say some parents do spend too much on toys, but the real problem is not spending itself — it is spending without enough thought. The best gift is not always the biggest pile of toys, but the one that keeps giving long after the excitement wears off.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說有些父母在玩具上確實花得太多，但真正的問題不在於花錢本身，而在於花得不夠有思考。最好的禮物未必是最大堆的玩具，而是那份在新鮮感退去之後，仍然能繼續帶來價值的東西。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say some parents do spend too much on toys, but the
real problem is not spending itself — it is spending without enough thought.
The best gift is not always the biggest pile of toys, but the one that keeps giving
long after the excitement wears off.
[總結來說，我會說有些父母在玩具上確實花得太多，但真正的問題不在於花錢本身，而在
於花得不夠有思考。最好的禮物未必是最大堆的玩具，而是那份在新鮮感退去之後，仍然能
繼續帶來價值的東西。]

question.english: When do people usually have parties in your country?
question.chinese: 在你所在的地方，人們通常會在甚麼時候開派對？

source_lines.text: Topic: When do people usually have parties in your country?
在你所在的地方，人們通常會在甚麼時候開派對？

components.english: In Hong Kong, people usually have parties when they are celebrating a special occasion, such as birthdays, weddings, graduations, or major festivals.
components.chinese: 在香港，人們通常會在慶祝一些特別場合時開派對，例如生日、婚禮、畢業，或者一些重要節日。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In Hong Kong, people usually have parties when they are celebrating a
special occasion, such as birthdays, weddings, graduations, or major festivals.
[在香港，人們通常會在慶祝一些特別場合時開派對，例如生日、婚禮、畢業，或者一些重要
節日。]

components.english: I think that is quite natural, because people tend to throw a party when they want to mark a moment and share the happiness with others. A party is not just about food and music; it is also a way of saying, “This matters, and I want the people around me to be part of it.” In that sense, parties are a bit like emotional bookmarks in life. They help people pause, celebrate, and remember.
components.chinese: 我覺得這其實很自然，因為當人們想為某一個時刻留個記號，並且想和身邊的人分享快樂時，就很容易會開派對。派對不只是食物和音樂而已；它同時也是一種在說：「這件事對我很重要，而我也想讓身邊的人一起參與。」從這個角度來看，派對有點像人生裡的情感書籤。它幫助人們停一停、慶祝一下，也把那個時刻記下來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: I think that is quite natural, because people tend to throw a
party when they want to mark a moment and share the happiness with others.
A party is not just about food and music; it is also a way of saying, “This
matters, and I want the people around me to be part of it.” In that sense, parties
are a bit like emotional bookmarks in life. They help people pause, celebrate,
and remember.
[我覺得這其實很自然，因為當人們想為某一個時刻留個記號，並且想和身邊的人分享快樂
時，就很容易會開派對。派對不只是食物和音樂而已；它同時也是一種在說：「這件事對我很
重要，而我也想讓身邊的人一起參與。」從這個角度來看，派對有點像人生裡的情感書籤。
它幫助人們停一停、慶祝一下，也把那個時刻記下來。]

components.english: For example, if someone has a birthday, gets married, or finishes university, it is quite common for them to invite friends and relatives to a restaurant, a function room, or even their home. In Hong Kong, wedding banquets in particular can be a pretty big deal, and birthday celebrations are also very common, especially among younger people.
components.chinese: 例如，如果有人生日、結婚，或者大學畢業，他們很常會邀請朋友和親戚去餐廳、宴會場地，甚至自己家裡一起慶祝。在香港，婚宴尤其可以算是相當隆重，而生日慶祝也非常普遍，尤其是在年輕人之間。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone has a birthday, gets married, or finishes
university, it is quite common for them to invite friends and relatives to a
restaurant, a function room, or even their home. In Hong Kong, wedding
banquets in particular can be a pretty big deal, and birthday celebrations are
also very common, especially among younger people.
[例如，如果有人生日、結婚，或者大學畢業，他們很常會邀請朋友和親戚去餐廳、宴會場地
，甚至自己家裡一起慶祝。在香港，婚宴尤其可以算是相當隆重，而生日慶祝也非常普遍，
尤其是在年輕人之間。]

components.english: So, generally speaking, people in Hong Kong tend to have parties when there is a personal milestone worth celebrating.
components.chinese: 所以，一般來說，香港人通常會在一些值得慶祝的人生時刻舉辦派對。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, generally speaking, people in Hong Kong tend to have parties
when there is a personal milestone worth celebrating.
[所以，一般來說，香港人通常會在一些值得慶祝的人生時刻舉辦派對。]

components.english: That said, people also have parties during holiday periods or social gatherings, even when there is no major life event behind them.
components.chinese: 不過，即使背後沒有甚麼重大人生事件，人們在節日期間或者一些社交聚會時，同樣也會開派對。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, people also have parties during holiday periods or social
gatherings, even when there is no major life event behind them.
[不過，即使背後沒有甚麼重大人生事件，人們在節日期間或者一些社交聚會時，同樣也會
開派對。]

components.english: During festive seasons, people are often more in the mood to let their hair down and spend time together. Holidays create a ready-made excuse to gather, eat well, and enjoy a more relaxed atmosphere. In a fast-paced place like Hong Kong, that kind of chance to unwind does not come along every day, so people tend to make the most of it when it does.
components.chinese: 到了節日氣氛比較濃的時候，人們通常也更有心情放鬆一下，和別人聚在一起。假期本身就像是一個現成的理由，讓大家可以相聚、好好吃一頓，並享受比較輕鬆的氣氛。在像香港這樣節奏很快的地方，那種真正能鬆一口氣的機會並不是天天都有，所以一旦有了，人們通常都會好好把握。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: During festive seasons, people are often more in the mood to let
their hair down and spend time together. Holidays create a ready-made excuse
to gather, eat well, and enjoy a more relaxed atmosphere. In a fast-paced place
like Hong Kong, that kind of chance to unwind does not come along every day,
so people tend to make the most of it when it does.
[到了節日氣氛比較濃的時候，人們通常也更有心情放鬆一下，和別人聚在一起。假期本身
就像是一個現成的理由，讓大家可以相聚、好好吃一頓，並享受比較輕鬆的氣氛。在像香港
這樣節奏很快的地方，那種真正能鬆一口氣的機會並不是天天都有，所以一旦有了，人們通
常都會好好把握。]

components.english: For instance, around Christmas, New Year, or sometimes after big company events, people may organise house parties, office parties, or dinners with friends. These may be less formal than weddings or banquets, but they still give people a chance to switch off, catch up, and have a good time together.
components.chinese: 例如，在聖誕節、新年左右，或者有時在一些大型公司活動之後，人們可能會安排家裡派對、公司派對，或者和朋友一起吃飯聚會。這些場合也許沒有婚宴或正式宴會那麼隆重，但仍然能讓人有機會放鬆下來、和別人聊聊天，並且一起開心一下。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, around Christmas, New Year, or sometimes after big
company events, people may organise house parties, office parties, or dinners
with friends. These may be less formal than weddings or banquets, but they still
give people a chance to switch off, catch up, and have a good time together.
[例如，在聖誕節、新年左右，或者有時在一些大型公司活動之後，人們可能會安排家裡派
對、公司派對，或者和朋友一起吃飯聚會。這些場合也許沒有婚宴或正式宴會那麼隆重，但
仍然能讓人有機會放鬆下來、和別人聊聊天，並且一起開心一下。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong usually have parties for two main reasons: to celebrate important moments and to enjoy time together during festive or social occasions. In other words, parties are often where celebration and connection meet.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說香港人通常開派對，主要有兩個原因：一是慶祝重要時刻，二是在節日或社交場合裡一起享受相聚的時間。換句話說，派對很多時候就是慶祝和連結彼此相遇的地方。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in Hong Kong usually have parties for
two main reasons: to celebrate important moments and to enjoy time together
during festive or social occasions. In other words, parties are often where
celebration and connection meet.
[總結來說，我會說香港人通常開派對，主要有兩個原因：一是慶祝重要時刻，二是在節日
或社交場合裡一起享受相聚的時間。換句話說，派對很多時候就是慶祝和連結彼此相遇的
地方。]

question.english: What makes a good party?
question.chinese: 甚麼才算是一個好的派對？

source_lines.text: Topic: What makes a good party?
甚麼才算是一個好的派對？

components.english: In my view, what makes a good party is not luxury, but good company and the right atmosphere.
components.chinese: 在我看來，一個好的派對重點不在於是否豪華，而在於有沒有好的人，以及對的氣氛。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In my view, what makes a good party is not luxury, but good company
and the right atmosphere.
[在我看來，一個好的派對重點不在於是否豪華，而在於有沒有好的人，以及對的氣氛。]

components.english: Even a beautiful venue and expensive food can fall flat if people feel awkward, left out, or unsure of themselves. What really makes a party work is a relaxed, welcoming mood where people can talk easily, laugh naturally, and let their hair down. In that sense, the atmosphere is the invisible glue that holds the whole thing together.
components.chinese: 即使場地很漂亮、食物很昂貴，如果大家感到尷尬、被冷落，或者不知道怎樣融入，那個派對仍然可能很失敗。真正令一個派對成功的，是一種放鬆而且令人感到受歡迎的氣氛，讓人可以自在交談、自然發笑，也能真正放鬆下來。從這個角度來看，氣氛就像是一種看不見的黏合劑，把整個聚會連在一起。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Even a beautiful venue and expensive food can fall flat if people
feel awkward, left out, or unsure of themselves. What really makes a party work
is a relaxed, welcoming mood where people can talk easily, laugh naturally, and
let their hair down. In that sense, the atmosphere is the invisible glue that holds
the whole thing together.
[即使場地很漂亮、食物很昂貴，如果大家感到尷尬、被冷落，或者不知道怎樣融入，那個派
對仍然可能很失敗。真正令一個派對成功的，是一種放鬆而且令人感到受歡迎的氣氛，讓人
可以自在交談、自然發笑，也能真正放鬆下來。從這個角度來看，氣氛就像是一種看不見的
黏合劑，把整個聚會連在一起。]

components.english: For example, even a simple gathering at someone’s home can be memorable if the guests get along well, the conversation flows, and no one feels like a spare part in the room. Sometimes a small party with warmth has far more sparkle than a huge event with no soul.
components.chinese: 例如，即使只是一個在家裡舉行的簡單聚會，如果賓客相處融洽、談話自然流動，而且沒有人覺得自己像房間裡多出來的那一個，那個聚會都可以非常難忘。有時一個有溫度的小聚會，反而比一場沒有靈魂的大型活動更有光彩。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, even a simple gathering at someone’s home can be
memorable if the guests get along well, the conversation flows, and no one feels
like a spare part in the room. Sometimes a small party with warmth has far
more sparkle than a huge event with no soul.
[例如，即使只是一個在家裡舉行的簡單聚會，如果賓客相處融洽、談話自然流動，而且沒
有人覺得自己像房間裡多出來的那一個，那個聚會都可以非常難忘。有時一個有溫度的小
聚會，反而比一場沒有靈魂的大型活動更有光彩。]

components.english: So, first and foremost, a good party depends on the feeling it creates rather than the money spent on it.
components.chinese: 所以，首先，一個好的派對最重要的是它營造出來的感覺，而不是花了多少錢。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, first and foremost, a good party depends on the feeling it
creates rather than the money spent on it.
[所以，首先，一個好的派對最重要的是它營造出來的感覺，而不是花了多少錢。]

components.english: On top of that, practical details matter a great deal, because a good party also needs to run smoothly.
components.chinese: 除此之外，實際安排也非常重要，因為一個好的派對同時也需要進行得順暢。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, practical details matter a great deal, because a good
party also needs to run smoothly.
[除此之外，實際安排也非常重要，因為一個好的派對同時也需要進行得順暢。]

components.english: Things like the right music, enough food, comfortable space, sensible timing, and a host who knows how to break the ice can make a huge difference. A party should not feel chaotic, but it should not feel stiff either. It needs to strike the right balance between structure and spontaneity. If the basics go wrong, the mood can unravel surprisingly quickly.
components.chinese: 像是合適的音樂、足夠的食物、舒適的空間、合理的時間安排，以及一位懂得帶動氣氛的主人，其實都可以帶來很大的分別。一個派對不應該亂成一團，但也不應該僵硬得像公式一樣。它需要在安排和自然之間取得恰到好處的平衡。如果基本部分出錯，整個氣氛往往會很快散掉。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Things like the right music, enough food, comfortable space,
sensible timing, and a host who knows how to break the ice can make a huge
difference. A party should not feel chaotic, but it should not feel stiff either. It
needs to strike the right balance between structure and spontaneity. If the basics
go wrong, the mood can unravel surprisingly quickly.
[像是合適的音樂、足夠的食物、舒適的空間、合理的時間安排，以及一位懂得帶動氣氛的
主人，其實都可以帶來很大的分別。一個派對不應該亂成一團，但也不應該僵硬得像公式一
樣。它需要在安排和自然之間取得恰到好處的平衡。如果基本部分出錯，整個氣氛往往會很
快散掉。]

components.english: For instance, if the music is too loud, the food arrives too late, or the guests do not know each other and nobody helps them connect, the evening can become painfully awkward. By contrast, when the host introduces people well and keeps things flowing, the party usually finds its own rhythm.
components.chinese: 例如，如果音樂太吵、食物太遲才到，或者賓客彼此不認識，而又沒有人幫忙打開話題，那個晚上就可能變得很尷尬。相反，當主人懂得替大家搭橋、讓整個流程自然推進時，派對通常就會慢慢找到自己的節奏。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, if the music is too loud, the food arrives too late, or
the guests do not know each other and nobody helps them connect, the evening
can become painfully awkward. By contrast, when the host introduces people
well and keeps things flowing, the party usually finds its own rhythm.
[例如，如果音樂太吵、食物太遲才到，或者賓客彼此不認識，而又沒有人幫
忙打開話題，那個晚上就可能變得很尷尬。相反，當主人懂得替大家搭橋、讓
整個流程自然推進時，派對通常就會慢慢找到自己的節奏。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say a good party is made by a mix of warm people and thoughtful planning. It does not have to be flashy to be unforgettable; it just has to make people feel comfortable, connected, and genuinely happy to be there.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說，一個好的派對是由有溫度的人，以及有心思的安排共同組成的。它不一定要很浮誇，才會令人難忘；它只需要讓人感到舒服、有連結，而且真心覺得自己很高興出現在那裡。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say a good party is made by a mix of warm people
and thoughtful planning. It does not have to be flashy to be unforgettable; it
just has to make people feel comfortable, connected, and genuinely happy to be
there.
[總結來說，我會說，一個好的派對是由有溫度的人，以及有心思的安排共同
組成的。它不一定要很浮誇，才會令人難忘；它只需要讓人感到舒服、有連結
，而且真心覺得自己很高興出現在那裡。]

question.english: What are the main reasons why people organise family parties in your country?
question.chinese: 在你地方，人們舉辦家庭聚會的主要原因是甚麼？

source_lines.text: What are the main reasons why people organise family parties in your country?
在你地方，人們舉辦家庭聚會的主要原因是甚麼？

components.english: In a place like Hong Kong, I think people mainly organise family parties to celebrate important occasions and, just as importantly, to bring the family together.
components.chinese: 在像香港這樣的地方，我認為人們舉辦家庭聚會，主要是為了慶祝重要場合，而同樣重要的是，把一家人聚在一起。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: In a place like Hong Kong, I think people mainly organise family parties
to celebrate important occasions and, just as importantly, to bring the family
together.
[在像香港這樣的地方，我認為人們舉辦家庭聚會，主要是為了慶祝重要場合，而同樣重要
的是，把一家人聚在一起。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern life is busy, fast, and often a little scattered, so family parties give people a reason to stop, gather, and reconnect. They are not only about food and celebration; they are also about keeping relationships warm. In that sense, a family party can act like glue: it holds people together when everyday life keeps pulling them in different directions.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，現代生活忙碌、節奏快，而且很多時有點零散，所以家庭聚會正好給人一個理由停下來、聚起來，重新連結。它不只是關於吃飯和慶祝，同時也是在維繫彼此之間的溫度。從這個角度來說，家庭聚會有點像膠水一樣：當日常生活一直把人往不同方向拉開時，它能把大家重新黏在一起。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that modern life is busy, fast, and often a
little scattered, so family parties give people a reason to stop, gather, and
reconnect. They are not only about food and celebration; they are also about
keeping relationships warm. In that sense, a family party can act like glue: it
holds people together when everyday life keeps pulling them in different
directions.
[主要原因是，現代生活忙碌、節奏快，而且很多時有點零散，所以家庭聚會正好給人一個理
由停下來、聚起來，重新連結。它不只是關於吃飯和慶祝，同時也是在維繫彼此之間的溫
度。從這個角度來說，家庭聚會有點像膠水一樣：當日常生活一直把人往不同方向拉開時，
它能把大家重新黏在一起。]

components.english: For example, people may organise family gatherings for birthdays, weddings, baby celebrations, or festivals such as Lunar New Year. Even relatives who hardly see one another during the year may still come together for a big meal, catch up, and share the occasion properly.
components.chinese: 例如，人們可能會因為生日、婚禮、孩子滿月，或者農曆新年這些節日而安排家庭聚會。即使一些親戚平時一年到頭都很少見面，到了這些場合，大家仍然會一起吃一頓飯、聊聊近況，並且好好地一起過那個日子。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, people may organise family gatherings for birthdays,
weddings, baby celebrations, or festivals such as Lunar New Year. Even relatives
who hardly see one another during the year may still come together for a big
meal, catch up, and share the occasion properly.
[例如，人們可能會因為生日、婚禮、孩子滿月，或者農曆新年這些節日而安排家庭聚會。即
使一些親戚平時一年到頭都很少見面，到了這些場合，大家仍然會一起吃一頓飯、聊聊近況
，並且好好地一起過那個日子。]

components.english: So, one major reason for family parties is simply to celebrate life’s important moments while keeping family ties alive.
components.chinese: 所以，家庭聚會其中一個很主要的原因，其實就是一邊慶祝人生中的重要時刻，一邊維繫家人之間的關係。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major reason for family parties is simply to celebrate life’s
important moments while keeping family ties alive.
[所以，家庭聚會其中一個很主要的原因，其實就是一邊慶祝人生中的重要時刻，一邊維繫
家人之間的關係。]

components.english: On top of that, I think family parties are also organised out of tradition and a sense of respect, especially towards older family members.
components.chinese: 除此之外，我也認為家庭聚會很多時是出於傳統，以及一種對長輩的尊重。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: On top of that, I think family parties are also organised out of
tradition and a sense of respect, especially towards older family members.
[除此之外，我也認為家庭聚會很多時是出於傳統，以及一種對長輩的尊重。]

components.english: To be honest, in many families, gathering is not just a personal choice; it is also part of family culture. Turning up for a reunion dinner, an elder’s birthday, or an important family event can be a way of showing that you still value the family bond. It is less about putting on a show and more about saying, without saying it directly, “You matter, and this family still matters.”
components.chinese: 老實說，在很多家庭裡，聚在一起不只是個人選擇，同時也是家庭文化的一部分。出席團年飯、長輩壽宴，或者一些重要家庭場合，很多時都是一種表達：你仍然重視這段家庭關係。這與其說是在做表面工夫，不如說是在用一種不必明講的方式表達：「你很重要，而這個家也仍然很重要。」

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, in many families, gathering is not just a personal
choice; it is also part of family culture. Turning up for a reunion dinner, an
elder’s birthday, or an important family event can be a way of showing that you
still value the family bond. It is less about putting on a show and more about
saying, without saying it directly, “You matter, and this family still matters.”
[老實說，在很多家庭裡，聚在一起不只是個人選擇，同時也是家庭文化的一部分。出席團年
飯、長輩壽宴，或者一些重要家庭場合，很多時都是一種表達：你仍然重視這段家庭關係。
這與其說是在做表面工夫，不如說是在用一種不必明講的方式表達：「你很重要，而這個家
也仍然很重要。」]

components.english: For instance, even if family members are busy with work or study, they may still make an effort to attend a grandparent’s birthday dinner or a festive reunion meal. Sometimes the gathering itself may be simple, but the gesture carries real weight.
components.chinese: 例如，即使家人平時工作或學業都很忙，他們很多時仍然會盡量抽時間出席祖父母的生日飯局，或者節日裡的團聚晚餐。有時候，聚會本身也許很簡單，但那份心意其實很有分量。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, even if family members are busy with work or study,
they may still make an effort to attend a grandparent’s birthday dinner or a
festive reunion meal. Sometimes the gathering itself may be simple, but the
gesture carries real weight.
[例如，即使家人平時工作或學業都很忙，他們很多時仍然會盡量抽時間出席祖父母的生日
飯局，或者節日裡的團聚晚餐。有時候，聚會本身也許很簡單，但那份心意其實很有分量。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say people in my place organise family parties mainly to celebrate, reconnect, and show care and respect. In the end, these gatherings are not just about having a good time for one evening; they are one of the ways a family keeps its sense of closeness alive.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說我這裡的人舉辦家庭聚會，主要是為了慶祝、重新連結，以及表達關心和尊重。說到底，這些聚會不只是為了熱鬧一個晚上；它們其實是讓一個家庭把那份親密感持續留住的其中一種方式。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say people in my place organise family parties
mainly to celebrate, reconnect, and show care and respect. In the end, these
gatherings are not just about having a good time for one evening; they are one
of the ways a family keeps its sense of closeness alive.
[總結來說，我會說我這裡的人舉辦家庭聚會，主要是為了慶祝、重新連結，以及表達關心和
尊重。說到底，這些聚會不只是為了熱鬧一個晚上；它們其實是讓一個家庭把那份親密感持
續留住的其中一種方式。]

question.english: In some places people spend a lot of money on parties that celebrate special family events. Is this ever true in your country? Do you think this is a good trend or a bad trend?
question.chinese: 有些地方的人會花很多錢去舉辦慶祝家庭特別事件的派對。這種情況在你的國家存在嗎？你認為這是好趨勢還是壞趨勢？

source_lines.text: Topic: In some places people spend a lot of money on parties that celebrate
special family events. Is this ever true in your country? Do you think this is a
good trend or a bad trend?
有些地方的人會花很多錢去舉辦慶祝家庭特別事件的派對。這種情況在你
的國家存在嗎？你認為這是好趨勢還是壞趨勢？

components.english: Yes, this is definitely true in Hong Kong, especially for occasions like weddings, milestone birthdays, baby full-month celebrations, or big family banquets. Personally, I think it can be a good thing in moderation, but it becomes a bad trend when people spend beyond their means.
components.chinese: 是的，這種情況在香港的確很常見，尤其是在婚禮、重要生日、嬰兒滿月酒，或者大型家庭宴會這些場合。就我個人而言，我認為適度地花錢可以是好事，但如果花到超出自己能力範圍，這就會變成一個不好的趨勢。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, this is definitely true in Hong Kong, especially for occasions like
weddings, milestone birthdays, baby full-month celebrations, or big family
banquets. Personally, I think it can be a good thing in moderation, but it
becomes a bad trend when people spend beyond their means.
[是的，這種情況在香港的確很常見，尤其是在婚禮、重要生日、嬰兒滿月酒，或者大型家庭
宴會這些場合。就我個人而言，我認為適度地花錢可以是好事，但如果花到超出自己能力範
圍，這就會變成一個不好的趨勢。]

components.english: The reason is that in Hong Kong, family events are often seen as once-in-a-lifetime moments, so people want to make them look special and memorable. A celebration is not just about food or decoration; it is also about face, family pride, and showing respect to relatives and guests. In that sense, people are not only throwing a party; they are putting on a moment that says, “This matters to us.”
components.chinese: 原因在於，在香港，家庭大事很多時都被視為一生一次的重要時刻，所以人們自然想把它辦得特別一點，也值得回憶。這類慶祝不只是關於食物或佈置，很多時也和面子、家庭榮耀，以及對親友賓客的尊重有關。從這個角度來看，人們不只是辦一場派對，而是在呈現一個訊息：「這件事對我們很重要。」

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The reason is that in Hong Kong, family events are often seen as
once-in-a-lifetime moments, so people want to make them look special and
memorable. A celebration is not just about food or decoration; it is also about
face, family pride, and showing respect to relatives and guests. In that sense,
people are not only throwing a party; they are putting on a moment that says,
“This matters to us.”
[原因在於，在香港，家庭大事很多時都被視為一生一次的重要時刻，所以人們自然想把它
辦得特別一點，也值得回憶。這類慶祝不只是關於食物或佈置，很多時也和面子、家庭榮耀
，以及對親友賓客的尊重有關。從這個角度來看，人們不只是辦一場派對，而是在呈現一個
訊息：「這件事對我們很重要。」]

components.english: For example, some families may book a hotel ballroom for a wedding, prepare an elaborate banquet, hire a photographer, and make sure everything looks polished from start to finish. Even for a child’s birthday or a full-month party, some parents really go all out because they want the day to feel unforgettable.
components.chinese: 例如，有些家庭會為婚禮預訂酒店宴會廳、安排很講究的酒席、聘請攝影師，並確保整個流程從頭到尾都做得體體面面。即使只是小朋友的生日會，或者嬰兒滿月酒，有些家長也真的會全力以赴，因為他們希望那一天感覺特別難忘。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, some families may book a hotel ballroom for a
wedding, prepare an elaborate banquet, hire a photographer, and make sure
everything looks polished from start to finish. Even for a child’s birthday or a
full-month party, some parents really go all out because they want the day to feel
unforgettable.
[例如，有些家庭會為婚禮預訂酒店宴會廳、安排很講究的酒席、聘請攝影師，並確保整個
流程從頭到尾都做得體體面面。即使只是小朋友的生日會，或者嬰兒滿月酒，有些家長也真
的會全力以赴，因為他們希望那一天感覺特別難忘。]

components.english: So yes, this is certainly true in Hong Kong, and I can understand why people do it, because family celebrations often carry emotional and social weight at the same time.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，這種情況在香港確實存在，而我也能理解人們為甚麼會這樣做，因為家庭慶祝很多時同時承載著情感上的意義和社交上的分量。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, this is certainly true in Hong Kong, and I can understand
why people do it, because family celebrations often carry emotional and social
weight at the same time.
[所以，是的，這種情況在香港確實存在，而我也能理解人們為甚麼會這樣做，因為家庭慶
祝很多時同時承載著情感上的意義和社交上的分量。]

components.english: That said, if I had to choose, I would say it is more of a bad trend when the spending becomes excessive or competitive.
components.chinese: 不過，如果一定要選一邊，我會說，當花費變得過度，甚至帶有攀比意味時，這更像是一個壞趨勢。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, if I had to choose, I would say it is more of a bad trend
when the spending becomes excessive or competitive.
[不過，如果一定要選一邊，我會說，當花費變得過度，甚至帶有攀比意味時，這更像是一個
壞趨勢。]

components.english: The problem is that celebrations can easily stop being about joy and start becoming about appearances. When people feel pressured to keep up with others, what should be a warm family occasion can turn into a financial burden. In other words, the party may look dazzling on the outside, but behind the scenes it can leave stress, debt, and unnecessary pressure. A special day should bring people together, not leave them picking up the pieces afterwards.
components.chinese: 問題在於，慶祝活動很容易由單純的開心，慢慢變成關於排場和表面風光的事情。當人們感到要跟別人看齊時，本來應該溫暖的家庭場合，就可能變成一種經濟負擔。換句話說，派對表面上也許光鮮亮麗，但背後卻可能留下壓力、負債，以及不必要的負擔。特別的日子本來應該讓人更親近，而不是讓人事後還要收拾殘局。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The problem is that celebrations can easily stop being about
joy and start becoming about appearances. When people feel pressured to keep
up with others, what should be a warm family occasion can turn into a financial
burden. In other words, the party may look dazzling on the outside, but behind
the scenes it can leave stress, debt, and unnecessary pressure. A special day
should bring people together, not leave them picking up the pieces afterwards.
[問題在於，慶祝活動很容易由單純的開心，慢慢變成關於排場和表面風光的事情。當人們
感到要跟別人看齊時，本來應該溫暖的家庭場合，就可能變成一種經濟負擔。換句話說，派
對表面上也許光鮮亮麗，但背後卻可能留下壓力、負債，以及不必要的負擔。特別的日子本
來應該讓人更親近，而不是讓人事後還要收拾殘局。]

components.english: For instance, a couple may spend a huge amount on a wedding simply because they do not want it to look “less impressive” than someone else’s. They may get one glamorous evening, but then spend months worrying about the bill. In that case, the celebration ends up costing more than money; it also costs peace of mind.
components.chinese: 例如，一對新人可能會在婚禮上花很多錢，只是因為他們不想讓自己的婚禮看起來比別人遜色。結果，他們也許得到了一個很華麗的晚上，卻可能之後好幾個月都在為帳單煩惱。在這種情況下，這場慶祝所付出的代價就不只是金錢，還包括內心的安穩。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, a couple may spend a huge amount on a wedding
simply because they do not want it to look “less impressive” than someone else’s.
They may get one glamorous evening, but then spend months worrying about
the bill. In that case, the celebration ends up costing more than money; it also
costs peace of mind.
[例如，一對新人可能會在婚禮上花很多錢，只是因為他們不想讓自己的婚禮看起來比別人
遜色。結果，他們也許得到了一個很華麗的晚上，卻可能之後好幾個月都在為帳單煩惱。在
這種情況下，這場慶祝所付出的代價就不只是金錢，還包括內心的安穩。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say spending money on special family events is understandable and can be meaningful, but only up to a point. In the end, what matters most is not how grand the occasion looks, but whether it is filled with love, blessings, and sincerity. If people can celebrate warmly without turning the occasion into a contest, then it is a healthy tradition; if not, it becomes more show than substance.
components.chinese: 總結來說，在家庭特別日子上花錢是可以理解的，也可以很有意義，但一切都應該有個限度。說到底，真正重要的不是場面有多大，而是那個場合是否充滿愛、祝福和真心。如果人們能夠溫暖地慶祝，而不是把那個場合變成一場比拼，那它就是一種健康的傳統；否則，它就會變得重形式、多於重實質。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say spending money on special family events is
understandable and can be meaningful, but only up to a point. In the end, what
matters most is not how grand the occasion looks, but whether it is filled with
love, blessings, and sincerity. If people can celebrate warmly without turning the
occasion into a contest, then it is a healthy tradition; if not, it becomes more
show than substance.
[總結來說，在家庭特別日子上花錢是可以理解的，也可以很有意義，但一切都應該有個限
度。說到底，真正重要的不是場面有多大，而是那個場合是否充滿愛、祝福和真心。如果人
們能夠溫暖地慶祝，而不是把那個場合變成一場比拼，那它就是一種健康的傳統；否則，它
就會變得重形式、多於重實質。]

question.english: Why do some people think that national celebrations are a waste of government money? Do you agree or disagree with this view? Why?
question.chinese: 為甚麼有些人認為國家慶祝活動是在浪費政府金錢？你同意還是不同意這個看法？為甚麼？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do some people think that national celebrations are a waste of
government money? Do you agree or disagree with this view? Why?
為甚麼有些人認為國家慶祝活動是在浪費政府金錢？你同意還是不同意這
個看法？為甚麼？

components.english: I can understand why some people think that way, because public money is limited and many people feel it should go to urgent needs first.
components.chinese: 我可以理解為甚麼有些人會這樣想，因為公共資源本來就有限，而很多人都覺得政府應該先把錢用在較迫切的需要上。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I can understand why some people think that way, because public money
is limited and many people feel it should go to urgent needs first.
[我可以理解為甚麼有些人會這樣想，因為公共資源本來就有限，而很多人
都覺得政府應該先把錢用在較迫切的需要上。]

components.english: The main reason is that national celebrations can look expensive, symbolic, and a little removed from everyday struggle. If ordinary people are worried about housing, healthcare, education, or the cost of living, then fireworks, parades, and large public events may seem like dressing the window while the house still needs repair. In other words, some people feel the government should fix the roof before hanging the flags.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，國家慶祝活動很多時看起來花費不少，而且偏重象徵意義，和一般人的日常困難之間似乎有點距離。如果普通市民正在為住屋、醫療、教育，或者生活成本而煩惱，那麼煙花、巡遊，以及大型公共活動就可能令人覺得像是在櫥窗上做裝飾，但房子本身其實還需要修理。換句話說，有些人會覺得，政府應該先把屋頂修好，再來掛旗。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that national celebrations can look
expensive, symbolic, and a little removed from everyday struggle. If ordinary
people are worried about housing, healthcare, education, or the cost of living,
then fireworks, parades, and large public events may seem like dressing the
window while the house still needs repair. In other words, some people feel the
government should fix the roof before hanging the flags.
[主要原因是，國家慶祝活動很多時看起來花費不少，而且偏重象徵意義，和
一般人的日常困難之間似乎有點距離。如果普通市民正在為住屋、醫療、教
育，或者生活成本而煩惱，那麼煙花、巡遊，以及大型公共活動就可能令人覺
得像是在櫥窗上做裝飾，但房子本身其實還需要修理。換句話說，有些人會
覺得，政府應該先把屋頂修好，再來掛旗。]

components.english: For example, if a city is facing long hospital waiting times or a shortage of public housing, some citizens may see a lavish national event and think, “That money could have been spent somewhere more useful.” From their point of view, celebration can start to look less like unity and more like misplaced priority.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個城市正面對公立醫院輪候時間過長，或者公共房屋不足的問題，有些市民看到一場鋪張的國家慶祝活動時，就可能會想：「那筆錢明明可以花在更有用的地方。」從他們的角度來看，慶祝活動就可能不再像凝聚人心，反而更像是本末倒置。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a city is facing long hospital waiting times or a
shortage of public housing, some citizens may see a lavish national event and
think, “That money could have been spent somewhere more useful.” From their
point of view, celebration can start to look less like unity and more like
misplaced priority.
[例如，如果一個城市正面對公立醫院輪候時間過長，或者公共房屋不足的
問題，有些市民看到一場鋪張的國家慶祝活動時，就可能會想：「那筆錢明明
可以花在更有用的地方。」從他們的角度來看，慶祝活動就可能不再像凝聚人
心，反而更像是本末倒置。]

components.english: So, from that perspective, I think the criticism is understandable, because people want public spending to ease real pressure rather than simply create a grand occasion.
components.chinese: 所以，從這個角度來看，我認為這種批評是可以理解的，因為人們希望公共開支能真正紓緩現實壓力，而不只是製造一場盛大的場合。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, from that perspective, I think the criticism is understandable,
because people want public spending to ease real pressure rather than simply
create a grand occasion.
[所以，從這個角度來看，我認為這種批評是可以理解的，因為人們希望公共
開支能真正紓緩現實壓力，而不只是製造一場盛大的場合。]

components.english: That said, I do not fully agree that national celebrations are simply a waste of money, because they can serve an important social and cultural purpose if they are planned sensibly.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不完全同意國家慶祝活動就只是浪費金錢，因為如果規劃得宜，它們其實可以有重要的社會和文化意義。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not fully agree that national celebrations are simply a
waste of money, because they can serve an important social and cultural purpose
if they are planned sensibly.
[不過，我並不完全同意國家慶祝活動就只是浪費金錢，因為如果規劃得宜，
它們其實可以有重要的社會和文化意義。]

components.english: A country is not held together by roads and budgets alone. It also needs shared memory, public spirit, and a sense that people belong to something larger than themselves. National celebrations can create that feeling. They can bring people together, honour history, and give citizens a rare moment to pause and reflect on who they are as a community. To me, the real issue is not whether celebrations should exist, but whether they are meaningful and proportionate. If they become all glitter and no substance, then the criticism is fair. But if they are thoughtful, inclusive, and not overblown, they can be money well spent.
components.chinese: 一個國家並不是只靠道路和預算維繫起來的。它同時也需要共同記憶、公共精神，以及一種「人們屬於某個更大整體」的感覺。國家慶祝活動可以創造這種感受。它們能把人聚在一起、紀念歷史，也能讓市民難得地停一停，想一想自己作為一個社群的一部分到底是誰。對我來說，真正的問題不在於慶祝活動應不應該存在，而在於它是否有意義、是否合乎分寸。如果它變成只有表面的熱鬧、卻沒有內涵，那批評當然合理；但如果它是有思考的、有包容性的，而且不過分鋪張，那它其實可以是花得值得的錢。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A country is not held together by roads and budgets alone. It
also needs shared memory, public spirit, and a sense that people belong to
something larger than themselves. National celebrations can create that feeling.
They can bring people together, honour history, and give citizens a rare moment
to pause and reflect on who they are as a community. To me, the real issue is not
whether celebrations should exist, but whether they are meaningful and
proportionate. If they become all glitter and no substance, then the criticism is
fair. But if they are thoughtful, inclusive, and not overblown, they can be money
well spent.
[一個國家並不是只靠道路和預算維繫起來的。它同時也需要共同記憶、公
共精神，以及一種「人們屬於某個更大整體」的感覺。國家慶祝活動可以創造
這種感受。它們能把人聚在一起、紀念歷史，也能讓市民難得地停一停，想一
想自己作為一個社群的一部分到底是誰。對我來說，真正的問題不在於慶祝
活動應不應該存在，而在於它是否有意義、是否合乎分寸。如果它變成只有
表面的熱鬧、卻沒有內涵，那批評當然合理；但如果它是有思考的、有包容性
的，而且不過分鋪張，那它其實可以是花得值得的錢。]

components.english: For instance, if a national celebration includes community performances, local artists, cultural events, and activities that families can genuinely take part in, it can feel less like a spectacle imposed from above and more like a shared civic moment. In that case, the value is not only in the event itself, but in the feeling of connection it leaves behind.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一場國家慶祝活動包括社區表演、本地藝術家、文化節目，以及家庭能真正參與的活動，那它給人的感覺就不會只是自上而下的表演，而更像是一個共同參與的公民時刻。在這種情況下，它的價值不只在活動本身，也在於它留下來的那份連結感。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if a national celebration includes community
performances, local artists, cultural events, and activities that families can
genuinely take part in, it can feel less like a spectacle imposed from above and
more like a shared civic moment. In that case, the value is not only in the event
itself, but in the feeling of connection it leaves behind.
[例如，如果一場國家慶祝活動包括社區表演、本地藝術家、文化節目，以及
家庭能真正參與的活動，那它給人的感覺就不會只是自上而下的表演，而更
像是一個共同參與的公民時刻。在這種情況下，它的價值不只在活動本身，
也在於它留下來的那份連結感。]

components.english: Overall, I would say I partly disagree with the view that national celebrations are a waste of government money. If a government neglects people’s real needs and spends recklessly, then yes, such events can look wasteful. But if celebrations are modest, meaningful, and balanced against public needs, they can strengthen national identity and social cohesion in a way that is worth paying for.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我對「國家慶祝活動是在浪費政府金錢」這個看法是部分不同意的。我認為問題不在於慶祝本身，而在於分寸和優先次序。如果政府忽視了人民真正的需要，卻又胡亂花錢，那麼這些活動當然會顯得浪費。不過，如果慶祝活動規模適中、有真正意義，而且和公共需要之間保持平衡，那麼它們其實可以以一種值得投資的方式，去加強國家認同和社會凝聚力。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say I partly disagree with the view that
national celebrations are a waste of government money. If a government neglects
people’s real needs and spends recklessly, then yes, such events can look wasteful.
But if celebrations are modest, meaningful, and balanced against public needs,
they can strengthen national identity and social cohesion in a way that is worth
paying for.
[總結來說，我對「國家慶祝活動是在浪費政府金錢」這個看法是部分不同意的。我認為問題
不在於慶祝本身，而在於分寸和優先次序。如果政府忽視了人民真正的需要，卻又胡亂花錢
，那麼這些活動當然會顯得浪費。不過，如果慶祝活動規模適中、有真正意義，而且和公共
需要之間保持平衡，那麼它們其實可以以一種值得投資的方式，去加強國家認同和社會凝
聚力。]

question.english: How important is it to celebrate important events with a group of people?
question.chinese: 和一群人一起慶祝重要時刻有多重要？

source_lines.text: How important is it to celebrate important events with a group of people?
和一群人一起慶祝重要時刻有多重要？

components.english: I think it is quite important, because big moments in life often feel more special when they are shared.
components.chinese: 我認為這是相當重要的，因為人生裡那些重要時刻，很多時候在和別人一起分享之後，才會顯得更特別。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think it is quite important, because big moments in life often feel more
special when they are shared.
[我認為這是相當重要的，因為人生裡那些重要時刻，很多時候在和別人一起分享之後，才
會顯得更特別。]

components.english: The main reason is that happiness usually grows when it has somewhere to go. If something wonderful happens and you celebrate it with people who care about you, the whole moment feels warmer, fuller, and more alive. It is not just about the event itself; it is also about the feeling of being seen, supported, and celebrated by others. In that sense, joy often lands more deeply when it has company.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，快樂很多時候在有地方可以流向別人時，才會變得更大。如果有好事發生，而你又能和那些真心在乎你的人一起慶祝，整個時刻就會變得更溫暖、更完整，也更有生命力。那不只是關於那件事本身，同時也關於那種被看見、被支持、被祝福的感覺。從這個角度來看，喜悅很多時候在有人陪伴時，才會更真正落進心裡。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that happiness usually grows when it has
somewhere to go. If something wonderful happens and you celebrate it with
people who care about you, the whole moment feels warmer, fuller, and more
alive. It is not just about the event itself; it is also about the feeling of being
seen, supported, and celebrated by others. In that sense, joy often lands more
deeply when it has company.
[主要原因是，快樂很多時候在有地方可以流向別人時，才會變得更大。如果有好事發生，
而你又能和那些真心在乎你的人一起慶祝，整個時刻就會變得更溫暖、更完整，也更有生命
力。那不只是關於那件事本身，同時也關於那種被看見、被支持、被祝福的感覺。從這個角
度來看，喜悅很多時候在有人陪伴時，才會更真正落進心裡。]

components.english: For example, if someone graduates, gets engaged, or lands their dream job, they may of course feel happy on their own. But if they celebrate it with family or close friends, the memory often becomes much richer. Years later, people may forget the exact words, but they still remember the laughter, the faces, and the feeling in the room.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人畢業、訂婚，或者得到夢想工作，他當然自己也會很開心。但如果他能和家人或親密朋友一起慶祝，那段回憶很多時候就會變得豐富得多。很多年後，人們也許會忘記當時說過甚麼，但仍然會記得那天的笑聲、那些面孔，以及整個空間裡的感覺。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone graduates, gets engaged, or lands their
dream job, they may of course feel happy on their own. But if they celebrate it
with family or close friends, the memory often becomes much richer. Years later,
people may forget the exact words, but they still remember the laughter, the
faces, and the feeling in the room.
[例如，如果一個人畢業、訂婚，或者得到夢想工作，他當然自己也會很開心。但如果他能和
家人或親密朋友一起慶祝，那段回憶很多時候就會變得豐富得多。很多年後，人們也許會忘
記當時說過甚麼，但仍然會記得那天的笑聲、那些面孔，以及整個空間裡的感覺。]

components.english: So yes, I would say celebrating with other people is important, because it turns a happy moment into a shared memory.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說和別人一起慶祝是重要的，因為它能把一個快樂時刻變成一段共同的回憶。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say celebrating with other people is important,
because it turns a happy moment into a shared memory.
[所以，是的，我會說和別人一起慶祝是重要的，因為它能把一個快樂時刻變成一段共同的
回憶。]

components.english: That said, I do not think an important event has to be celebrated with a big crowd to matter.
components.chinese: 不過，我並不認為重要時刻一定要和一大群人慶祝，才算有意義。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think an important event has to be celebrated with
a big crowd to matter.
[不過，我並不認為重要時刻一定要和一大群人慶祝，才算有意義。]

components.english: To be honest, the right people matter more than a large number of people. Some celebrations are better when they are quiet, simple, and personal. If the group gets too big, the moment can sometimes lose its heart and start to feel more like a performance than a real celebration. So, for me, the key is not size but sincerity. A small circle can sometimes mean far more than a noisy room full of people who barely know the story behind the moment.
components.chinese: 老實說，對的人比起人多更重要。有些慶祝其實更適合安靜、簡單，而且帶一點私人感。如果人太多，那個時刻有時反而會失去原本的溫度，開始變得像一場表演，而不再是一場真正的慶祝。所以，對我來說，關鍵不在人數，而在真誠。有時候，一個小小的圈子，反而比一個擠滿了卻沒幾個真正知道背後故事的房間，更有分量。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, the right people matter more than a large
number of people. Some celebrations are better when they are quiet, simple, and
personal. If the group gets too big, the moment can sometimes lose its heart and
start to feel more like a performance than a real celebration. So, for me, the key
is not size but sincerity. A small circle can sometimes mean far more than a noisy
room full of people who barely know the story behind the moment.
[老實說，對的人比起人多更重要。有些慶祝其實更適合安靜、簡單，而且帶一點私人感。如
果人太多，那個時刻有時反而會失去原本的溫度，開始變得像一場表演，而不再是一場真正
的慶祝。所以，對我來說，關鍵不在人數，而在真誠。有時候，一個小小的圈子，反而比一個
擠滿了卻沒幾個真正知道背後故事的房間，更有分量。]

components.english: For instance, someone might celebrate passing an important exam with just their parents and one close friend over dinner. That may sound small, but it can still feel deeply meaningful, because the people at the table are the ones who truly walked the road with them.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能只是和父母以及一位很親密的朋友吃一頓飯，來慶祝自己通過重要考試。這聽起來也許不算盛大，但仍然可以非常有意義，因為坐在桌前的那些人，正正就是一路陪他走過來的人。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone might celebrate passing an important exam
with just their parents and one close friend over dinner. That may sound small,
but it can still feel deeply meaningful, because the people at the table are the
ones who truly walked the road with them.
[例如，一個人可能只是和父母以及一位很親密的朋友吃一頓飯，來慶祝自己通過重要考
試。這聽起來也許不算盛大，但仍然可以非常有意義，因為坐在桌前的那些人，正正就是一
路陪他走過來的人。]

components.english: Overall, I would say it is important to celebrate major events with other people, but not necessarily with a large group. What really gives a celebration its weight is not the crowd, but the closeness, warmth, and genuine feeling in it.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說和別人一起慶祝重要時刻是重要的，但不一定非要一大群人才行。真正令一場慶祝有份量的，不是場面有多大，而是當中的親密感、溫度，以及那份真實的情感。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I would say it is important to celebrate major events
with other people, but not necessarily with a large group. What really gives a
celebration its weight is not the crowd, but the closeness, warmth, and genuine
feeling in it.
[總結來說，我會說和別人一起慶祝重要時刻是重要的，但不一定非要一大群人才行。真正
令一場慶祝有份量的，不是場面有多大，而是當中的親密感、溫度，以及那份真實的情感。]

question.english: What decisions do people generally make in their daily life?
question.chinese: 人們在日常生活中一般會作出哪些決定？

source_lines.text: Topic: What decisions do people generally make in their daily life?
人們在日常生活中一般會作出哪些決定？

components.english: I think people make countless small practical decisions every day, especially about time, money, food, and routine.
components.chinese: 我認為，人們每天都會作出數不清的小型實際決定，特別是關於時間、金錢、飲食，以及日常安排這幾方面。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think people make countless small practical decisions every day,
especially about time, money, food, and routine.
[我認為，人們每天都會作出數不清的小型實際決定，特別是關於時間、金錢、飲食，以及日
常安排這幾方面。]

components.english: From the moment people wake up, they are already making choices. They decide what time to get up, what to wear, what to eat, how to get to work or school, what to prioritise first, and how much money to spend. Most of these decisions may look ordinary, but they are the nuts and bolts of daily life. In a way, an ordinary day is really just a long chain of tiny crossroads.
components.chinese: 從人們一醒來開始，其實就已經在作選擇了。他們要決定幾點起床、穿甚麼、吃甚麼、怎樣去上班或上學、先處理甚麼事情，以及要花多少錢。這些決定很多時候看起來很平凡，但它們其實正是日常生活運作的基本零件。某程度上，一個普通日子本身，其實就是一連串細小十字路口組成的。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: From the moment people wake up, they are already making
choices. They decide what time to get up, what to wear, what to eat, how to get
to work or school, what to prioritise first, and how much money to spend. Most
of these decisions may look ordinary, but they are the nuts and bolts of daily
life. In a way, an ordinary day is really just a long chain of tiny crossroads.
[從人們一醒來開始，其實就已經在作選擇了。他們要決定幾點起床、穿甚麼、吃甚麼、怎樣
去上班或上學、先處理甚麼事情，以及要花多少錢。這些決定很多時候看起來很平凡，但它
們其實正是日常生活運作的基本零件。某程度上，一個普通日子本身，其實就是一連串細小
十字路口組成的。]

components.english: For example, a person may decide whether to cook at home or eat out, whether to take the bus or a taxi, and whether to save money or buy something they want straight away. None of those choices sounds dramatic, but together they shape how the day unfolds.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人可能要決定是在家煮飯還是外出吃、坐巴士還是搭的士，以及把錢存起來還是立刻買下自己想要的東西。這些選擇單看都不算戲劇化，但加起來卻會決定整天是怎樣展開的。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a person may decide whether to cook at home or eat
out, whether to take the bus or a taxi, and whether to save money or buy
something they want straight away. None of those choices sounds dramatic, but
together they shape how the day unfolds.
[例如，一個人可能要決定是在家煮飯還是外出吃、坐巴士還是搭的士，以及把錢存起來還
是立刻買下自己想要的東西。這些選擇單看都不算戲劇化，但加起來卻會決定整天是怎樣
展開的。]

components.english: So, one major kind of decision people make every day is practical decision-making, because daily life runs on small choices more than big speeches.
components.chinese: 所以，人們每天作出的其中一大類決定，就是實際層面的決定，因為日常生活很多時靠的不是甚麼大道理，而是這些細小選擇。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major kind of decision people make every day is practical
decision-making, because daily life runs on small choices more than big speeches.
[所以，人們每天作出的其中一大類決定，就是實際層面的決定，因為日常生活很多時靠的
不是甚麼大道理，而是這些細小選擇。]

components.english: At the same time, people also make emotional and social decisions every day, and those can matter just as much.
components.chinese: 與此同時，人們每天其實也會作出情感上和社交上的決定，而這些決定同樣可以非常重要。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: At the same time, people also make emotional and social decisions
every day, and those can matter just as much.
[與此同時，人們每天其實也會作出情感上和社交上的決定，而這些決定同樣可以非常重

components.english: Daily life is not only about schedules and spending; it is also about how people deal with other people. They decide how to speak to others, whether to be patient or impatient, whether to forgive, whether to help, whether to stay quiet or speak up, and how much energy to give to the people around them. These choices are quieter, but they often leave a deeper footprint. A careless word can spoil a day, while a kind one can light it up.
components.chinese: 日常生活不只關乎時間表和花費，它同時也關乎人們怎樣面對別人。人們要決定怎樣和別人說話、選擇有耐性還是沒耐性、要不要原諒、要不要幫忙、是保持沉默還是開口表達，以及要把多少心力放在身邊的人身上。這些選擇雖然較安靜，但很多時候反而會留下更深的痕跡。一句不小心的話可以毀掉一整天，而一句善意的話卻能把整天照亮。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: Daily life is not only about schedules and spending; it is also
about how people deal with other people. They decide how to speak to others,
whether to be patient or impatient, whether to forgive, whether to help,
whether to stay quiet or speak up, and how much energy to give to the people
around them. These choices are quieter, but they often leave a deeper footprint.
A careless word can spoil a day, while a kind one can light it up.
[日常生活不只關乎時間表和花費，它同時也關乎人們怎樣面對別人。人們要決定怎樣和別
人說話、選擇有耐性還是沒耐性、要不要原諒、要不要幫忙、是保持沉默還是開口表達，以
及要把多少心力放在身邊的人身上。這些選擇雖然較安靜，但很多時候反而會留下更深的
痕跡。一句不小心的話可以毀掉一整天，而一句善意的話卻能把整天照亮。]

components.english: For instance, someone may come home tired and have to decide whether to snap at their family or calm themselves down first. Another person may see a friend struggling and decide whether to reach out or simply mind their own business. Those decisions may not appear on any to-do list, but they often say the most about a person’s character.
components.chinese: 例如，一個人下班回家很累時，可能就要決定自己是把情緒發洩在家人身上，還是先讓自己冷靜下來。另一個人看到朋友正處於困境時，也可能要決定自己是主動伸出援手，還是只顧好自己就算了。這些決定不會出現在任何待辦清單上，但它們很多時候最能反映一個人的品格。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone may come home tired and have to decide
whether to snap at their family or calm themselves down first. Another person
may see a friend struggling and decide whether to reach out or simply mind
their own business. Those decisions may not appear on any to-do list, but they
often say the most about a person’s character.
[例如，一個人下班回家很累時，可能就要決定自己是把情緒發洩在家人身上，還是先讓自
己冷靜下來。另一個人看到朋友正處於困境時，也可能要決定自己是主動伸出援手，還是只
顧好自己就算了。這些決定不會出現在任何待辦清單上，但它們很多時候最能反映一個人
的品格。]

components.english: Overall, I would say people generally make two kinds of decisions in daily life: practical ones and personal ones. The practical choices keep life moving, while the personal ones give that life its tone and texture.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們在日常生活中一般會作出兩大類決定：一類是實際的，另一類是個人的。實際上的選擇讓生活繼續運轉，而個人層面的選擇，則決定了那種生活的溫度和質感。
